attrs.alt: Azure AD B2C User Flow — Sign-up and Sign-in

content.text: User flows (formerly called built-in policies) are pre-built, configurable authentication journeys in Azure AD B2C. They define what happens when a customer signs up, signs in, resets their password, or edits their profile — without requiring you to write XML policy code. User flows are configured through the Azure Portal and generate an OIDC/OAuth 2.0 endpoint that your application calls.

User flows vs custom policies: user flows are the recommended starting point for most applications. Custom policies (Identity Experience Framework / IEF) are necessary only when you need logic that user flows cannot express — step-up authentication, A/B testing, or deeply custom claims transformations.
Combines registration and login in one flow
Login only — assumes account already exists
Lets signed-in users update their attributes
Self-service password reset via email OTP
Required with local accounts; link from sign-in error page
Phone number as primary identifier with SMS OTP
Creating a Sign-up and Sign-in User Flow
In the Azure Portal, switch to your B2C tenant directory.
Navigate to Azure AD B2C → Policies → User flows → New user flow.
Select 'Sign up and sign in' → Recommended (V2).
Choose identity providers: Email signup and/or social IdPs you configured.
Under User attributes, select attributes to collect during sign-up (Display name, City, etc.).
Under Application claims, select which attributes to include in the returned token.
Name the flow (e.g. SignUpSignIn) — it becomes B2C_1_SignUpSignIn.

Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contoso.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'Policy.ReadWrite.TrustFramework'

# Create a combined sign-up/sign-in user flow
$flow = @{
    '@odata.type' = '#microsoft.graph.b2cIdentityUserFlow'
    id            = 'B2C_1_SignUpSignIn'
    userFlowType  = 'signUpOrSignIn'
    userFlowTypeVersion = 2
}

Invoke-MgGraphRequest `
    -Method POST `
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/b2cUserFlows' `
    -Body ($flow | ConvertTo-Json) `
    -ContentType 'application/json'

Write-Host 'User flow B2C_1_SignUpSignIn created.'

Each user flow type has multiple versions. Microsoft releases new versions with security fixes and UX improvements. The portal labels versions as 'Recommended' or 'Classic'. Always create new flows on the Recommended version — Classic versions lack features like MFA improvements and updated UI templates.
V2 includes combined page layout and reCAPTCHA support
Only available as Recommended; GA since 2022
Attributes are the data points collected from the user and stored in their B2C profile. Two distinct settings control each attribute:
User attributes (collect): shown to the user on the sign-up page so they can enter values.
Application claims (return): included in the JWT token sent back to the application.
An attribute can be collected but not returned in the token, or pre-populated and returned without prompting the user.
Adding a Custom Attribute to a User Flow via Graph

Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contoso.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'Policy.ReadWrite.TrustFramework'

# First, create the custom attribute at the tenant level
$attr = @{
    displayName  = 'loyaltyTier'
    description  = 'Customer loyalty tier'
    dataType     = 'string'
}
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/userFlowAttributes' `
    -Body ($attr | ConvertTo-Json) -ContentType 'application/json'

# Then link it to the user flow as a user attribute (collect) and application claim (return)
# Get the attribute ID
$attrObj = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/userFlowAttributes' |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty Value |
    Where-Object { $_.displayName -eq 'loyaltyTier' }

$flowId = 'B2C_1_SignUpSignIn'

# Add as a user attribute (shown on sign-up page)
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/b2cUserFlows/$flowId/userAttributeAssignments" `
    -Body (@{
        isOptional           = $true
        requiresVerification = $false
        userInputType        = 'DropdownSingleSelect'
        displayName          = 'Loyalty Tier'
        userAttribute        = @{ id = $attrObj.Id }
        userFlowId           = $flowId
    } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5) -ContentType 'application/json'

Write-Host 'Custom attribute loyaltyTier added to user flow.'
Configuring MFA in a User Flow
Multi-factor authentication can be enabled per user flow. B2C supports SMS/phone call OTP and TOTP (authenticator app). MFA can be set to Always-on, Conditional, or Off.

# Update user flow to require MFA on every sign-in
$flowId = 'B2C_1_SignUpSignIn'

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PATCH `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/b2cUserFlows/$flowId" `
    -Body (@{
        isLanguageCustomizationEnabled = $true
        mfaConfiguration = @{
            clientTimeoutInSeconds = 300
            # 'off' | 'onSignIn' | 'onSignUpOrInitialSignInOnly' | 'ifRequired'
            mfaState = 'onSignIn'
            mfaTypes = @('phone')
        }
    } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5) -ContentType 'application/json'

Write-Host "MFA set to 'onSignIn' for $flowId"

The Azure Portal provides a built-in 'Run user flow' button that launches the sign-in page in the browser, letting you verify the flow end-to-end without wiring up an application. The test tool generates an authorization URL using the test application registered by B2C.

# OIDC authorization request targeting a specific user flow
# Replace <tenant>, <client_id>, <redirect_uri>, and <flow_name> with your values

TENANT='contoso.onmicrosoft.com'
CLIENT_ID='<your-app-client-id>'
REDIRECT_URI='https%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A5001%2Fsignin-oidc'
FLOW='B2C_1_SignUpSignIn'
NONCE=$(openssl rand -hex 16)
STATE=$(openssl rand -hex 16)

echo "https://${TENANT}.b2clogin.com/${TENANT}/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?"
echo "  p=${FLOW}"
echo "  &client_id=${CLIENT_ID}"
echo "  &nonce=${NONCE}"
echo "  &redirect_uri=${REDIRECT_URI}"
echo "  &scope=openid%20offline_access"
echo "  &response_type=code"
echo "  &state=${STATE}"

Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contoso.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'Policy.ReadWrite.TrustFramework'

# List all user flows
$flows = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/b2cUserFlows' |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty Value

foreach ($f in $flows) {
    Write-Host "$($f.id) | $($f.userFlowType) v$($f.userFlowTypeVersion)"
}

# Delete a user flow (cannot delete if apps are currently using it)
$flowToDelete = 'B2C_1_OldSignUpSignIn'
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method DELETE `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/b2cUserFlows/$flowToDelete"
Write-Host "Deleted: $flowToDelete"
p= parameter in auth request does not match any flow ID
Check flow ID in portal; ensure the B2C_1_ prefix is included
Sign-in page shows no social IdP buttons
IdP registered in tenant but not linked to this specific user flow
Add the IdP to the user flow under Identity providers
Custom attribute not shown on sign-up page
Attribute created at tenant level but not added to flow's user attribute assignments
Assign the attribute to the user flow with userInputType set
Attribute collected but not added to Application claims
Edit the user flow and enable the claim under Application claims
MFA prompt appears every sign-in despite 'ifRequired'
Conditional Access policy is marking every session as high-risk
Check CA policies in the B2C tenant; exclude low-risk sign-ins

B2C user flows support UI text customization for 36 languages. You can override any string shown on the sign-in, sign-up, MFA, or error pages — product name, button labels, error messages, and accessibility strings.

# Language customization must be enabled on the user flow first
$flowId = 'B2C_1_SignUpSignIn'

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PATCH `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/b2cUserFlows/$flowId" `
    -Body (@{ isLanguageCustomizationEnabled = $true } | ConvertTo-Json) `
    -ContentType 'application/json'
Write-Host 'Language customization enabled.'

# Override the 'Sign in' button label for English
$flowId  = 'B2C_1_SignUpSignIn'
$locale  = 'en'
$pageType = 'unifiedssp'   # unified sign-in/sign-up page

# The override JSON — key names match the page's resource keys
$overrideJson = @{
    LocalizedStrings = @(
        @{ ElementType = 'UxElement'; ElementId = $null; StringId = 'button_signin'; Override = $true; Value = 'Log In' },
        @{ ElementType = 'UxElement'; ElementId = $null; StringId = 'social_intro'; Override = $true; Value = 'Sign in with your account' }
    )
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

# Convert to bytes and PUT
$bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($overrideJson)

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PUT `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/b2cUserFlows/$flowId/languages/$locale/overridesPages/$pageType" `
    -Body $bytes `
    -ContentType 'application/json'
Write-Host 'Language override applied for English unifiedssp page.'

API connectors let you call an external REST endpoint at specific points in the user flow — for example, to validate a user's email domain during sign-up or to enrich the token claims with data from an external system.
Enrich token with data from a CRM; add loyalty tier claim
After the user authenticates with a social IdP
Validate the user's email against an allow-list
During sign-up, before the account is written to directory
Verify age, validate promo code, enforce domain restrictions
Registering and Linking an API Connector

Connect-MgGraph -TenantId 'contoso.onmicrosoft.com' -Scopes 'APIConnectors.ReadWrite.All'

# Step 1: Create the connector definition
$connector = @{
    displayName     = 'EnrichTokenClaims'
    targetUrl       = 'https://api.contoso.com/b2c/enrich-claims'
    authenticationConfiguration = @{
        '@odata.type' = '#microsoft.graph.basicAuthentication'
        username      = 'b2c-connector'
        password      = '<secure-password>'
    }
}

$connectorObj = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/apiConnectors' `
    -Body ($connector | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5) -ContentType 'application/json'

Write-Host "Connector ID: $($connectorObj.id)"

# Step 2: Link connector to the 'Before sending the token' point in a user flow
$flowId = 'B2C_1_SignUpSignIn'
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PUT `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/b2cUserFlows/$flowId/apiConnectorConfiguration/postTokenIssuanceStart" `
    -Body (@{ '@odata.id' = "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/apiConnectors/$($connectorObj.id)" } | ConvertTo-Json) `
    -ContentType 'application/json'
Write-Host 'API connector linked to postTokenIssuanceStart.'
Auditing User Flow Activity with KQL
KQL: Sign-in Events by User Flow

// Count sign-in attempts by user flow over the last 7 days
SignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| extend FlowName = tostring(AdditionalDetails[0].value)
| where FlowName startswith 'B2C_1'
| summarize TotalSignIns = count(), Failures = countif(ResultType != 0)
    by FlowName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName has_any ('Add user flow', 'Update user flow', 'Delete user flow',
    'Add API connector to user flow', 'Update identity providers for user flow')
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    OperationName,
    Actor = InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName,
    FlowName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName),
    Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc
The AzureAD Terraform provider does not yet have a dedicated azuread_b2c_user_flow resource as of version 2.x. Instead, use the azapi provider which maps directly to ARM and Graph API resources.
B2C user flows are pre-built, configurable authentication journeys for sign-up/sign-in, profile editing, and password reset — no XML required.
User flows are referenced in OIDC authorization requests via the p= parameter (e.g. p=B2C_1_SignUpSignIn).
Always create flows on the Recommended version (V2 for sign-up/sign-in) to get the latest security and UX improvements.
User attributes control what data is collected during sign-up; application claims control what appears in the returned token.
MFA can be enabled per flow with modes: off, onSignIn, onSignUpOrInitialSignInOnly, or ifRequired.
The Graph API endpoint /identity/b2cUserFlows enables programmatic management of all user flow configuration.
